title: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South - Clive Lewis
h1: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South
text_excerpt: Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South - Clive Lewis Skip to main content Clive Lewis For Norwich South Join Get Involved Donate Latest News About Clive Commentary and Analysis Can you help me and Labour in Norwich? Campaigns Standing up for the victims of the Infected Blood Scandal Backing a more inclusive education system for SEND children Campaigning for decent, accessible dental services in Norwich TOGETHER WE SAVED OUR WALK-IN CENTRE! Campaigning for decent mental health care in Norwich and Norfolk Standing up for our city’s military veterans Stop Redundancies at the University of East Anglia Campaigning to protect and improve public services in Norwich Say NO to parking unfairness at Earlham house Save our Services Keep our Schools and Children Safe from Fire Save our Post Offices Scambassador Save Our Community Pharmacies Save our Pharmacy! Lloyds Pharmacy – Colman Road Join Labour’s campaign to SAVE OUR NHS Contact Clive Privacy Policy More Search Search the website Search Menu Clive Lewis, Labour MP for Norwich South Clive Lewis MP speaking at a local event Listening to you, it’s clear we share the same desire for a fully funded public, not private, NHS; for decent, secure, properly paid jobs; an economy that works for all of us, not just a few and where the wealthy pay their way. Here in Norwich, I’ve campaigned against the privatisation of local NHS services, been at the centre of a broad coalition of trade unions, faith groups, businesses and academics working for a Living Wage across the city, and worked with local campaign groups and trade unions for public ownership of the failing privatised rail industry. I’m passionate about Norwich’s development of scientific centres of excellence. I support institutions such as the Norwich Research Park, which aims to develop the city’s future by creating and supporting new companies and jobs based on world-leading bioscience and renewable technology. But despite all that, I’m not your typical politician. My life outside of politics has given me experience of the real world. For more than a decade, I was a BBC TV news reporter, eventually becoming the BBC eastern region’s chief political reporter. After that, I worked as a BBC technician. I’ve also been an army reservist infantry officer, serving a tour of duty in Afghanistan in 2009. I’m also a governor of Lakenham Primary School and Nursery in my constituency and have worked with other schools and charities to help some of our city’s most disadvantaged children. I was also one of the last Labour Government’s National Black Role Models, working with young people across the UK. Born in London, I grew up on a council estate in Northampton. I attended a local comprehensive, becoming the first family member to attend university. I read economics at the University of Bradford before being elected student union president and Vice President of the National Union of Students (NUS). Ultimately, I’ll always speak my mind and try to do what is best for Norwich and you can read more about my local and regional priorities here .
